stories.headline: Nuggets rout Trail Blazers 128-112 behind Jokic triple-double, Peyton Watson's return
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets earned their second consecutive victory Sunday afternoon with a 128-112 blowout of the Portland Trail Blazers at Ball Arena. Nikola Jokic recorded his 85th career triple-double with 22 points, 14 rebounds and 14 assists, while Jamal Murray added 22 points and Cam Johnson contributed 19.

Peyton Watson made his long-awaited return after missing six weeks with a right hamstring injury, playing just under 20 minutes on a medical restriction and finishing with 14 points, six rebounds and three assists. Coach David Adelman praised Watson's effort while urging patience with the young forward's workload management going forward.

Denver shot 54.4% from the field and distributed 37 assists as a team, with seven players scoring in double figures. The Nuggets' defense tightened after halftime, holding Portland to 43 second-half points, as Denver continues to position itself for the NBA playoffs with 10 regular-season games remaining.

stories.headline: Avalanche beat Capitals 3-2 in OT as Landeskog returns, Ovechkin scores 1,000th career goal
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche defeated the Washington Capitals 3-2 in overtime Sunday in Washington, D.C., with Brock Nelson scoring the winner 82 seconds into 3-on-3 overtime off a pass from Martin Necas. The victory extended Colorado's winning streak to two games following their playoff clinch on Friday.

Captain Gabe Landeskog returned to the lineup after missing seven games with a groin injury, providing an immediate lift to a team that is 36-4-7 when he plays compared to 9-9-3 when he does not. Gabriel Landeskog and Nicolas Roy scored Colorado's regulation goals.

The game also featured a historic milestone for the opposing side, as Alex Ovechkin scored his 1,000th career NHL goal — combining regular season and playoffs — to tie the game late in regulation. Ovechkin now stands at 923 regular-season goals, a record he set last year by surpassing Wayne Gretzky. Mackenzie Blackwood earned his 20th win of the season for Colorado, stopping 20 of 22 shots.

stories.headline: Rockies announce full opening day roster and starting rotation ahead of Friday opener in Miami
stories.summary:
Colorado Rockies manager Warren Schaeffer announced the club's opening day roster and starting rotation Sunday, finalizing the team's makeup ahead of their season opener Friday in Miami. Kyle Freeland will make a franchise-record fifth opening day start, followed in the rotation by Michael Lorenzen, Jose Quintana, Tomoyuki Sugano and Ryan Feltner.

Highly touted right-hander Chase Dollander, who posted a 6.52 ERA in 21 starts as a rookie last season, will begin 2026 in the bullpen and is expected to be the first rotation call-up in the event of injury or poor performance. First baseman T.J. Rumfield, acquired from the Yankees in January, claimed the starting job at first base after hitting .286 with four spring training home runs while Kris Bryant remains sidelined with ongoing back issues.

One roster spot remains undecided pending the health of utility player Tyler Freeman, who has been hampered by a back injury during spring training. The Rockies also optioned catcher Braxton Fulford to Triple-A Albuquerque and reassigned several players to minor-league camp, leaving 36 active players in big-league camp. Colorado's home opener is set for April 3 at Coors Field against the Philadelphia Phillies.

stories.headline: One person killed in multi-vehicle crash that shut down northbound I-25 in Lone Tree for hours
stories.summary:
At least one person died early Sunday morning in a multi-vehicle crash on northbound Interstate 25 near Lincoln Avenue in Lone Tree, prompting a closure that lasted several hours and snarled traffic in Douglas County. The city of Lone Tree posted the closure alert at approximately 4:28 a.m., and northbound lanes did not reopen until after 10 a.m., according to the Colorado Department of Transportation.

The Douglas County Sheriff's Office assisted Lone Tree police with the investigation. The number of vehicles involved and the identities of those affected had not been publicly released as of Sunday. The deceased will be identified by the Douglas County Coroner's Office.

The cause of the crash remains under investigation. Authorities asked drivers to avoid the area and seek alternate routes during the closure. The incident is the latest in a series of serious crashes affecting major Denver metro highways in recent weeks.

stories.headline: Denver's homicides fell nearly 48% in 2025, but violent crime rose in some neighborhoods
stories.summary:
Denver recorded just 37 homicides in 2025, a nearly 48% drop from 2024 and the largest year-over-year decline since 1990, resulting in the city's lowest homicide total in over a decade. Mayor Mike Johnston attributed the improvement to targeted policing, increased community engagement, technology tools and Place Network Investigation sites — a strategy that pairs police with city agencies and community stakeholders in high-crime areas.

Despite the citywide improvement, crime trends were uneven across Denver's neighborhoods. Data shows that violent and overall crime in the Union Station and northern Capitol Hill areas rose nearly 5.5% from 2024 to 2025, bucking the broader downward trend.

Johnston highlighted the La Alma-Lincoln Park neighborhood as a flagship success, noting the area had gone 452 days without a shooting as of late January. The mayor set 2026 goals of a 10% decrease in gun-related homicides and a 20% reduction of shootings in designated high-risk areas. Analysts also credited recent state legislation with contributing to the overall decline.

stories.headline: Colorado 'No Kings' protests grow to 72 events statewide ahead of March 28 demonstrations
stories.summary:
At least 72 anti-Trump "No Kings" protest events are now scheduled across Colorado for March 28, up from 43 events listed for the prior round of demonstrations on March 4, according to online listings published March 20. The events span more than 60 cities and towns, including Denver, Aurora, Boulder, Colorado Springs, Fort Collins, Greeley and dozens of smaller communities statewide.

Organizers say the protests challenge the Trump administration's use of executive power, federal law enforcement and other actions they characterize as authoritarian overreach. This month's wave was specifically prompted by the actions of Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers in Minnesota, including the killings of two American citizens during enforcement operations.

Previous rounds of "No Kings" protests in Colorado, held in June and October 2025, drew tens of thousands of participants. The ACLU of Colorado has published a guide to protesters' legal rights on its website ahead of the March 28 events.

stories.headline: Colorado Sun catalogs 10 visible signs of climate change already unfolding across the state
stories.summary:
The Colorado Sun published an in-depth report Sunday cataloging ten observable signs of climate change already affecting Colorado, ranging from record-low snowpack and unprecedented drought conditions to dying ponderosa pine forests and heat-related labor impacts on workers. State data shows Colorado is experiencing its warmest year in 131 years of recordkeeping, and average temperatures have risen 2.3 degrees Fahrenheit over the past 50 years.

Several Front Range and Western Slope communities have already imposed unusual restrictions in response: Loveland banned golf carts from dried-out courses, Thornton implemented strict summer watering limits, and ski resort workers have had hours cut due to reduced snowfall. Governor Jared Polis recently reactivated the state's drought task force.

Climate researchers and state officials interviewed for the report stressed the need for adaptation while cautioning against fatalism. Adrienne Marshall, a climate professor at Colorado School of Mines, noted that future warming levels still depend significantly on societal decisions about carbon emissions. State Forester Matt McCombs described the situation as "an end of innocence" for Colorado's forests, while urging communities to focus on practical adaptation rather than blame.

stories.headline: Globeville's Panadería Emmanuel endures as cultural anchor in Denver neighborhood facing gentrification pressure
stories.summary:
Panadería Emmanuel, a family-owned Mexican bakery operating for more than 30 years in Denver's Globeville neighborhood, continues to serve as a community gathering place for a historically working-class area tucked below Interstate 70. Owner Daniel Casillas — born in 1982 to a family that immigrated from Mexico — carries on baking traditions passed down from his father and grandfather, while his grandmother prepares menudo every weekend for neighbors who describe the food as a connection to home.

The bakery has become a meeting point for activists and community organizations, including Sidewalk Poets, a nonprofit serving writers from underserved backgrounds. Longtime resident Angela Garcia and others use the space to connect Globeville's youth with its elders, preserving neighborhood history amid rapid changes.

Globeville faces ongoing development pressure, with new apartment buildings rising between older homes and vacant lots bearing "for sale" signs. Community activists have raised concerns about a proposed data center and describe the changes not as gentrification but as displacement. The neighborhood's history includes the construction of I-70, which physically divided Globeville from the adjacent Elyria and Swansea neighborhoods.

stories.headline: Partial government shutdown leaves Denver-area TSA workers without full pay for more than a month
stories.summary:
Transportation Security Administration officers at Denver International Airport and airports nationwide have been working without full pay for more than 36 days as a partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security drags on. More than 120,000 DHS employees, including roughly 50,000 TSA officers, are impacted by the funding lapse, which stems from a congressional impasse over immigration enforcement funding.

Nonprofits and unions across the country have stepped in to provide food and financial assistance, though federal ethics rules prohibit TSA workers from directly accepting gifts at screening locations. Union organizations such as AFGE locals can receive donations on behalf of members and are directing those who want to help to their local chapters or labor councils.

AFGE Local 554 President Aaron Barker said affected officers have described being unable to pay utility bills, cover children's medical costs, or avoid eviction and vehicle repossession. The current shutdown follows a 43-day shutdown earlier this year — the longest in U.S. history — which also strained federal workers and food banks across Colorado and the nation.

weather.notable: About 21 degrees above the average high for this time of year!

joke.joke: A day without sunshine is like, you know, night.

topStory.headline: Two Pilots Killed as Air Canada Regional Jet Collides With Fire Truck on LaGuardia Runway
topStory.summary:
Two pilots were killed and 41 people were hospitalized late Sunday night after an Air Canada Express regional jet collided with a Port Authority fire-and-rescue vehicle on a runway at New York's LaGuardia Airport. The aircraft, a CRJ-900 operated by Jazz Aviation on behalf of Air Canada, had arrived from Montreal and landed on Runway 4 at approximately 11:45 p.m. local time when the collision occurred. The plane carried 72 passengers and four crew members.

The pilot and co-pilot aboard Flight AC8646 were killed when the nose of the aircraft struck the middle of the fire truck, absorbing the full force of the impact. The two Port Authority officers riding in the truck's cab were thrown from the vehicle but survived with non-life-threatening injuries. Of the 41 people taken to hospitals — 39 from the plane and two from the truck — Port Authority Executive Director Kathryn Garcia said 32 had been discharged by Monday morning, though some sustained serious injuries. A flight attendant was found outside the aircraft still strapped into her jumpseat after the collision.

The fire-and-rescue vehicle was responding to a separate incident involving United Airlines Flight 2384, whose crew had aborted a takeoff after an anti-ice warning light activated and reported an odor in the cabin, eventually declaring an emergency. Air traffic control recordings indicate the fire truck had received clearance to cross the runway before being told to stop. In a recorded exchange with a Frontier Airlines pilot, an air traffic controller said, "I was here. I tried to reach out to my staff, and we were dealing with an emergency earlier and I messed up."

LaGuardia Airport was closed following the collision, with the FAA issuing a ground stop through at least 2 p.m. Monday. Hundreds of flights across multiple carriers — including American Airlines, Delta, Southwest, United, and Air Canada — were cancelled or delayed. The National Transportation Safety Board launched an investigative team to the scene, and the FAA said it would support that investigation. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy issued a statement offering condolences and pledging continued coordination with the NTSB.

Investigators are focused on how both the landing aircraft and the fire truck received simultaneous clearances to occupy the same runway. Air Canada established a dedicated hotline — 1-800-961-7099 — for those seeking information about passengers aboard the flight. Authorities said no foul play or terrorism is suspected, and the incident is being treated as an accident.
